description_long: This is a brass trumpet with a classic cylindrical body and a flared bell at one end. The surface is metallic with a slightly mottled, aged brass appearance, giving it a vintage look. Three piston valves are positioned centrally along the top, each with a round button for finger operation. The mouthpiece is attached at the narrow end, opposite the bell, and the leadpipe curves gently into the main tuning slide, which is U-shaped and located just before the valve section. The instrument features a network of tubing that loops back on itself, characteristic of standard trumpet design. The bell is moderately wide and flares out smoothly, providing a distinct visual and acoustic profile.
description: A vintage brass trumpet with a flared bell, three piston valves, and a gently curved leadpipe. The surface is metallic and slightly mottled, with classic trumpet tubing and a U-shaped tuning slide.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the trumpet, the flared bell, three piston valves, and the looping tubing.
description_view_1: Front view looking directly into the bell, with the circular opening and valve tops visible behind.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the trumpet's length, bell flare, and valve arrangement from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the mouthpiece end and the alignment of the tubing and valves from behind.
attribute_bell_flare: The bell is moderately wide with a smooth, gradual flare.
attribute_valve_configuration: Three piston valves are aligned in a row along the top center of the trumpet.
attribute_leadpipe_curvature: The leadpipe is gently curved, connecting the mouthpiece to the main tuning slide with a smooth bend.
attribute_tuning_slide_location: The main tuning slide is U-shaped and located just before the valve section, oriented horizontally beneath the leadpipe.
